name: Pawn.vegas
description_short: Nevada Coin Mart is a Las Vegas-based second-hand dealer that buys items directly from consumers and offers quotes within 10 minutes, operating 365 days a year with 24/7 phone support.
description_long:
Nevada Coin Mart operates as a licensed second-hand dealer in Las Vegas, Nevada, specifically rejecting the pawn shop label despite functioning within that industry category. The company is located at a single address on S. Jones Boulevard across from the Post Office near an Arco station. Founded to serve consumers seeking to sell items directly for cash, Nevada Coin Mart positions itself as a buyer rather than a traditional lender, distinguishing its business model from conventional pawn operations that typically offer loans against collateral.

The company's primary service is purchasing secondhand items directly from consumers and paying competitive prices for those goods. They offer rapid quote assessments—claiming turnaround in 10 minutes or less—allowing sellers to understand item value before committing to a sale. Nevada Coin Mart emphasizes accessibility through multiple contact methods including phone, text, walk-ins, and email, with 24/7 availability via phone and text at 702-625-2111. The business maintains extended hours, operating 9am to 6pm every single day of the year without closures.

What distinguishes Nevada Coin Mart is its explicit branding as a second-hand dealer rather than a pawn shop, suggesting a focus on direct purchases rather than collateral-based lending. The company name references coins and precious metals, indicating specialization in high-value collectible items, though the website does not detail specific item categories accepted. The promise of "best price possible" and rapid quote delivery positions them as consumer-friendly compared to traditional pawn models. Their single-location operation suggests a locally-focused, established business rather than a franchise network.

The primary limitation is lack of transparency regarding what items they actually purchase, specific pricing formulas, or whether they offer loans at all. The website provides no details about condition requirements, valuation methodology, or the quote process beyond the 10-minute claim. For consumers seeking traditional pawn loans (borrowing against collateral), this buyer-focused model may not apply. The single location restricts accessibility to Las Vegas residents only.
address: 4065 S Jones Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89103

pros: 24/7 phone and text availability for immediate contact and quotes
Rapid quote turnaround of 10 minutes or less before committing to sale
Open 365 days per year, 7 days a week with consistent 9am-6pm hours
Explicitly licensed as a second-hand dealer, suggesting regulatory compliance
Direct purchase model eliminates loan repayment obligations
Multiple contact methods including phone, text, email, and in-person
cons: Website provides no details on what specific item categories are purchased
No information on valuation methodology, pricing formulas, or how quotes are calculated
Single Las Vegas location limits accessibility to local customers only
No transparency on minimum or maximum item values accepted
No condition requirements or item specifications publicly disclosed
best_for: Las Vegas residents seeking to sell collectible items or precious metals for immediate cash
Consumers with items to liquidate who want rapid valuation and same-day payment
Local sellers preferring direct purchase over traditional pawn loan agreements
diagnosis: Nevada Coin Mart is best for Las Vegas-area residents who own items (likely coins, precious metals, or collectibles based on company name) and want quick cash sales without loan obligations. The primary caveat is that the website provides almost no detail about what items they purchase, pricing methodology, or value ranges, requiring customers to contact them directly for any specific information.
services: Direct purchase of secondhand items from consumers
Rapid item valuation quotes (10 minutes or less)
In-person appraisal at S. Jones Boulevard location
